questionText:
Your teacher instructs you to edit a classmate's research paper. Which two tools
should you use? (Choose 2.)

options.label: A
options.text: Design mode

options.label: B
options.text: Spelling checker

options.label: C
options.text: Linked notes

options.label: D
options.text: Comments

questionText:
When using an app, network, or service, you may be required to agree to an
Acceptable Use Policy What are two types of information that an Acceptable Use Policy
describes? (Choose 2.)

options.label: A
options.text: The consequences of violating the policy

options.label: B
options.text: Unacceptable uses of the app, network, or service

options.label: C
options.text: How to copyright data that you submit to the app, network, or service

options.label: D
options.text: How to monetize information through the app, network, or service

questionText: What mental health issue can excessive social media use potentially lead to?

options.label: A
options.text: Improved social connections

options.label: B
options.text: Enhanced emotional resilience

options.label: C
options.text: Increased risk of mood swings

options.label: D
options.text: Heightened feelings of happiness

questionText: What is a benefit of "in private" or "incognito" browsing?

options.label: A
options.text: Your web browser allows you to change your IP address.

options.label: B
options.text: Your web browser permits visits to websites that are normally blocked in your country.

options.label: C
options.text: Your web browser blocks advertisements.

options.label: D
options.text: Your web browser hides activities from other people who use the same device

questionText:
For each statement about the Android operating system, select True or False.
Note: You will receive credit for each correct response.
Answer Area
Android is open-source software True
Android is a desktop operating system False
Android requires the Google App Store False

-- 1 of 21 --

What are two negative effects of posting a derogatory meme on social media?
(Choose 2)

options.label: A
options.text: It showcases your wit and digital artistry skills.

options.label: B
options.text: You can easily address a group of people who hold specific beliefs.

options.label: C
options.text: It remains traceable after the event that inspired you to post it.

options.label: D
options.text: Various viewers may interpret the meme differently

questionText:
You need to identify personally identifiable information (PII) that you should
not expose online. For each statement, select Yes if it is personally identifiable information or
No if it is not. Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.
Answer Area
Eye color False
Date of birth True
Passport number True

What web browser feature or setting should you use?

options.label: A

options.text:
History or Timeline

options.label: C

questionText:
You are conducting online research. You need to refine your search to return
results that are more relevant to your research subject. Which two techniques should you use?
(Choose 2.) Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.

options.label: A
options.text: Enclose multi-word search terms in quotation marks

options.label: B
options.text: Search for generic terms

options.label: C
options.text: Capitalize important term

options.label: D
options.text: Search for root words and exclude suffixes

questionText:
For each statement about time differences between wired and wireless
networking, select True or False. Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.
Answer Area
An encrypted Wi-Fi connection is more secure than an Ethernet connection False
Wireless networks typically have slower data transmission speeds than Ethernet connections
True
An Ethernet connection typically provides fast network connection speeds than a Wi-Fi
connection True

options.label: A

options.text: RAM

options.text:
Mbps

questionText:
You are researching information in a long document. For each statement,
select Yes if you can use the Find feature to complete the task, or No if you cannot. Note: You
will receive credit for each correct answer.
Answer Area
Locate a keyword or phrase within the document Yes
Change the format of all citations in your document No
Identify pages that include references by a specific author No

You have written an original recipe that you want to share on social media.
Your recipe includes a paragraph describing the dish, a list of ingredients, step-by-step
instructions, and screen photos. Which guideline will most likely impact where you are able to
share your recipe online?

options.label: A
options.text: Some social media sites have a limit on the number of characters or pictures you can post.

options.label: B
options.text: Some social media sites do not allow you to format information as a bulleted list.

options.label: C
options.text: Some social media sites do not allow you to post recipes.

options.label: D
options.text: Some social media sites have a limit on the length of videos you can post

questionText:
You enter a recipe into a Microsoft Word document as a single paragraph and
convert it to a numbered list, as shown in the image.
You need to separate the paragraph into individual numbered list items, as shown in the
image.
Which key (or key combination) should you press at the end of each sentence?

options.label: A
options.text: Shift+Tab

options.label: B
options.text: Tab

options.label: C
options.text: Shift+Enter

options.label: D
options.text:
Enter

questionText: Which setting will allow you to print multiple slides on one page of paper?

options.label: A
options.text: Pages per sheet

options.label: B
options.text: Slide layout

options.label: C
options.text: Double-sided or duplex

options.label: D
options.text: Slide sorter

questionText:
You need to maintain the security of your smartphone. Which three
guidelines should you follow? (Choose 3.) Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.

options.label: A
options.text: Disable Wi-Fi and Bluetooth when you're not using them.

options.label: B
options.text: Do not leave your device unattended in public or easily accessible areas.

options.label: C
options.text: Use a USB 3.0 or newer connection cable when connecting to a new computer.

options.label: D
options.text: Always use a surge protector when charging the device.

options.label: E
options.text:
Update your smartphone's operating system only if you need a new feature offered in the
update.
F. Avoid using public Wi-Fi networks.

questionText:
You need to follow ethical standards of behavior to protect your digital
reputation. Which action meets the requirements?

options.label: A
options.text: Use public domain images for your company website.

options.label: B
options.text: Include the full version of your favorite song in a personal video profile on social media.

options.label: C
options.text:
Comply with the terms of the Acceptable Use Policy while using your school's Wi-Fi.

-- 6 of 21 --

options.label: D
options.text:
Enhance the Work Experience section of your online resume by including tasks you intend to
complete

questionText:
For each statement about the benefits of a good network infrastructure, select
True or False. Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.
Answer Area
Ensures that students can consistently connect to the campus network True
Enables students to connect to the school's computer lab from any location False
Supports the scaling of the school network without redesign as the student population increases True

Your class is planning a field trip to visit college campuses. Your teacher
schedules a video conference for the trip participants using software that displays each attendee on
screen as shown.
You need to follow appropriate digital etiquette standards during the video conference.
For each statement, select Yes if it represents appropriate digital etiquette or No if it does not.
Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.
Answer Area
Test the software before the meeting start time Yes
Position yourself and the camera so the camera lens is at eye level Yes
Ensure that your microphone is always on so you can participate No
When it's your turn to speak, introduce yourself and look at the onscreen
image of the person you're addressing No

You use a public computer at the library to check email and your bank account
balance. You need to prevent other people who use the computer after you from accessing your
email and bank accounts. Which two tasks should you complete before leaving the computer?
(Choose 2.)

options.label: A
options.text: Clear the browser's cache and cookies

options.label: B
options.text:
Clear the browser's history

-- 7 of 21 --

options.label: C
options.text: Quit the browser sessions in which you're logged on to the websites

options.label: D
options.text: Log out of the email and bank websites

questionText:
You post a funny and embarrassing photo of your friend on social media How

options.label: A
options.text: Forever, even after you delete it

options.label: B
options.text: 7 years

options.label: C
options.text: 24 hours

options.label: D

questionText:
For each statement about open source software, select True or False. Note:
You will receive credit for each correct answer.
Answer Area
All free software is open source software False
Anyone can inspect, modify, and enhance the source code of open source software True

Your teacher instructs you to research a poster, a poem, a dramatic work, and an
image that are protected by any of the six Creative Commons licenses and are not dedicated to the
public domain. For each type of content use, select Yes if the terms of the Creative Commons
licenses allow the use of the work or No if they do not.
Answer Area
Display the poster during a free movie night at your school No
Include the poem as part of a portfolio of your original work Yes
Perform the dramatic work for parents at a class talent show Yes
Display the image to other students as part of an in-class presentation Yes

Which three devices output information when connected to a non-touchscreen
computer? (Choose 3.)
To answer, select the correct devices in the answer area. Note: You will receive credit for each
correct answer.
Correct Selections:
1. Monitor (The screen)
2. Printer (The device with paper)
3. Headphones (The audio device)
Which practice follows effective file-naming conventions?

options.label: A
options.text: Using the default file name

options.label: B
options.text: Using generic terms like "document" or "file"

options.label: C
options.text: Using clear names that describe the content

options.label: D

Including random letters and numbers for security

questionText:
Which two methods should you use to reduce eye strain while using a digital
display? (Choose 2.)

options.label: A
options.text: Periodically look away from the screen and focus on a faraway object.

options.label: B
options.text: Shine the primary room light source directly onto the screen.

options.label: C
options.text: Tilt the screen directly toward your eyes.

options.label: D
options.text: Adjust the screen resolution to set the text at a comfortable size.

questionText: What is a constructive reason for choosing not to engage with online trolling? I

options.label: A
options.text: It encourages the troll to continue their behavior

options.label: B
options.text: It motivates others to participate in the negative conversation

options.label: C
options.text: It helps avoid escalating the situation and reduces the troll's influence

options.label: D
options.text: It gives the troll an opportunity to explain their behavior.

questionText: What is spyware?

options.label: A
options.text: A program that is designed to look harmless but allows unauthorized access to your computer

options.label: B
options.text: A program that installs itself on your system without your knowledge and tracks your online habits

options.label: C
options.text: Content that tricks you into revealing account codes and passwords by pretending to be legitimate

options.label: D
options.text: A program that can copy itself and spread to other computers

questionText:
Which two actions can you take to help maintain your digital privacy? (Choose
2.)

options.label: A
options.text: Configure your web browser settings to block cookies

options.label: B
options.text: Store your private documents in a cloud storage location

options.label: C
options.text: Use only your school email to send private information

options.label: D
options.text: Turn off GPS on your devices when you are not actively using it

questionText:
Online messaging apps simplify the process of communicating with other people.
For each statement, select True or False. Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.

People who use smart-messaging share the same beliefs and practices whether they are next door or in
another country False
Communication through emojis reproduces social constructs such as stereotypes, prejudices, and
limitations True
Emojis are a constantly changing concept that may not universally reflect sources of study True

Move each data storage device from the list on the left to the correct definition on

Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.
A compact, lightweight external data storage device that uses flash memory USB flash drive
An external data storage device powered through the USB port of the computer Portable hard
drive

An internal electromechanical data storage device that stores and retrieves data using a rapidly rotating
platter Hard disk drive

Move each email address field from the list on the left to its purpose on the right.
Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.
To copy other people on a message and NOT let them see the email addresses Bcc
To copy other people on a message and let each see the email addresses Cc
To send the message you received from one person to a different person Forward
To respond to a message sender and exclude other message recipients Reply
To respond to a message sender and all other known message recipients Reply All

questionText:
You are writing a research paper. You need to add references to your work. For

Answer Area
You use an idea from a news article YES

Which statement correctly describes the use of Creative Commons content in a
project?

options.label: A
options.text:
All Creative Commons content is automatically owned by the government and cannot be used for
commercial purposes.

options.label: B
options.text:
Creative Commons licenses restrict the use of educational content and do not allow for personal or
commercial use.

options.label: C
options.text:
Creative Commons licenses allow you to use, share, and modify content as long as you follow the
terms specified by the license.

options.label: D
options.text: Creative Commons content can only be used if you pay a fee to the original creator for each use.

questionText:
Juliana takes photos with her phone for the school yearbook. She takes more
than 50 photographs each day. Juliana needs to use the quickest and most reliable method to back
up the photos so she can delete them from her phone each day. What should Juliana do?

options.label: A
options.text: Download the photos to a USB flash drive.

options.label: B
options.text: Email the photos to her teacher.

options.label: C
options.text: Sync her phone to cloud storage.

options.label: D
options.text: Delete only the photos she doesn't want to use.

questionText:
You are searching the web for information about how to grow your own
vegetables. You scan through several pages of results. You need to determine which results are
relevant to your search. For each webpage description, select Yes if the page is relevant to your
search or No if it is not. Note: You will receive credit for each correct answer.

A tabloid magazine article about celebrity gardens NO
A how-to article published by a prestigious university YES

options.label: A

options.label: B
options.text: Deleting files you don't need anymore

questionText:
For each statement about printing documents, select True or False.

You can change document margins from the Print settings False
Duplex printing prints file content on both sides of the paper True
You can only change the paper size from the Page Setup option False
To preserve file formatting when electronically distributing a document, print the document to a PDF file
True

options.label: A

options.label: C

questionText:
You are working with other students on a science project. Your team will
meet to discuss the project using the school's videoconferencing application. You will act as the
host. You need to ensure that each student can talk about the project without being interrupted by
other students. What should each student do to meet this goal?

options.label: A
options.text: Sign in early to verify that the audio and video technology works

options.label: B
options.text: Mute their microphone until you call on them

options.label: C
options.text: Remain on camera throughout the conference

options.label: D
options.text: Introduce themselves by name when they speak.

options.label: B
options.text: Facebook

options.label: C
options.text: Instagram

options.label: D

questionText:
Digital Primacy – True or False Statements
Answer Area
Updating your browser every six months wipes your digital footprint clean False
Anonymous online comments you post cannot be traced back to you if you use a web filter False
Potential employers can find images and messages posted on social media by applicants under the age of
18 True
Companies with which you share personal data in exchange for apps and services are not allowed to give
the data to anyone else False

Maddie uses social media to stay in touch with friends and family. She is planning
to go on vacation next month. What is the safest way for Maddie to protect herself from online
predators when posting information about her vacation activities?

options.label: A
options.text: Checking in online when she goes places so her parents know where she is

options.label: B
options.text: Posting her vacation plans before she leaves so her friends know she is out of town

options.label: C
options.text:
Setting her social media location map to "public"

-- 15 of 21 --

options.label: D
options.text: Waiting until she returns home to post vacation photos

options.text: Ethernet

questionText:
You see a post on social media that contains an article about a current event.
You want to evaluate the credibility of the article. Which factor is most important?

options.label: A
options.text: The number of pictures included in the article

options.label: B
options.text: The number of likes and shares the article has received

options.label: C
options.text: The length of the article and the details it covers

options.label: D
options.text: The news outlet's reputation and trustworthiness

questionText: When assessing an online article's reliability, which is a critical step?

options.label: A
options.text: Verifying that the article includes multiple images

options.label: B
options.text: Counting the article's words to determine depth of content

options.label: C
options.text: Reviewing the article's publication date and checking for recent updates

options.label: D
options.text: Confirming the author's popularity on social media

questionText:
You need to ensure the security of your passwords. Which three guidelines
should you follow? (Choose 3.)

options.label: A
options.text: Use the longest password or passphrase permissible by each password system.

options.label: B
options.text: Use passwords that are based on private personal information.

options.label: C
options.text: Use multi-factor authentication when available.

options.label: D
options.text: Use words that can be found in the dictionary of a language other than your primary language.

options.label: E
options.text:
Use complex passwords and record them in a notebook that you keep with you at all times.
F. Use a different password for each account.

questionText:
For each statement about Creative Commons, select True or False.
Answer Area
Allows creators to grant licenses to only specific people False
Helps facilitate the sharing and discovery of creative works on the web True
Provides tools that allow creators to dedicate their works to the public domain True

options.label: A
options.text: Use your name and birthday

options.label: B
options.text: Use your favorite movie title

options.label: C
options.text:
Use a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols

options.label: C

Answer Area
The webpage has misspelled words No
The webpage text is written objectively Yes

As an ethical computer user, you have a responsibility to practice good netiquette
at all times. You need to identify examples of good netiquette in an office work environment. For

Answer Area
Copy your coworkers on all email messages you send to keep them in the loop-
Yes
Share large files from a cloud storage location instead of attaching them to email messages - Yes
Apply the same standards and values to online interactions that you do to face-to-face interactions - Yes

You need to add artwork to a presentation you're creating for your class. You

Answer Area
The artist is your friend No
The artwork is protected by copyright No
The copyright owner dedicated the artwork to the public domain Yes

If you quote directly from a speech, you must cite the source True
If you summarize someone else's work, you must cite the source True
If you paraphrase someone else's work, you must cite the source True

Which two methods should you use to avoid computer-related injuries? (Choose 2.)

options.label: A

options.label: B

You are writing a research paper. You gather information from various sources
that you want to include in your paper. You need to reference your source material in your paper.
For which two reasons should you reference your source material? (Choose 2.)

options.label: A
options.text: It allows readers to find the original information source

options.label: B
options.text: It establishes the research paper as your original work

options.label: C
options.text: It gives credit to the people who did the research you're referencing

options.label: D
options.text: It provides financial payment to the people who performed the research

questionText: What is a computer virus?

options.label: A
options.text: A program that is designed to look harmless but allows unauthorized access to your computer

options.label: B
options.text: Content that tricks you into revealing account codes and passwords by pretending to be legitimate

options.label: C
options.text: A program that installs itself on your system without your knowledge and tracks your computer usage

options.label: D
options.text: A program that can copy itself and spread to other computers
